reasoning: A pandemic declaration by the WHO is a very rare event, historically occurring roughly once every 10 to 20 years (e.g., 2009 H1N1, 2020 COVID-19). This yields an annual base rate around 2% to 5%. In the absence of live search tools to verify events between January and October 2025, the most rational approach is to rely on the base rate. Furthermore, the WHO is typically conservative about formally declaring a 'pandemic', preferring Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC) unless global unrestricted spread is occurring. Thus, I assign a 2% probability.
